fix: net_r_realized includes full trade outcome + engine-specific PTP params

Two bugs in simulate_trade_outcome():

1. net_r_realized = realized_r (PTP portion only)
   Should be total_r (PTP + remaining position result)
   Effect: losses showed -0.05R (commission only) instead of -1.05R
           wins showed 0.45R (PTP) instead of actual total outcome
   Fix: replace round(realized_r,3) -> round(total_r,3) on all exit paths

2. PTP params hardcoded to engine_params['judas_swing']
   Should use signal.engine_name to get correct engine's partial_tp_rr/vol
   Effect: ict_enigma (ptp_rr=1.75) and ict_venom (ptp_rr=1.0) both used
           judas_swing defaults instead of their optimizer-tuned values
   Fix: ep = settings.engine_params.get(signal.engine_name, {})

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: :wrench: switch from MySQL to MariaDB in test workflow

Replace the ad-hoc 'sudo systemctl start mysql' setup with a proper
mariadb:lts service container. This matches the production/local
MariaDB environment and fixes two failing e2e tests that 500'd due to
MySQL 8 vs MariaDB incompatibility in the withApiary JSON_OBJECT query.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: 3D grid visualization, interactive 2D topology, SSE streaming

3D Command View (/grid-3d):
- Interactive 3D topology with Three.js/React Three Fiber
- Station-specific models: generator (arc reactor core, cooling tower,
  smokestack with animated steam), solar farm (20 tilted PV panels,
  inverter), load substations (HV transformers, surge arresters)
- Catenary physics transmission lines (y = a·cosh(x/a))
- Animated power flow particles (sphere-based, speed ∝ power magnitude)
- Click-to-inspect: detailed telemetry/status overlay panel
- Hover effects with subtle scale animation
- Pulsing status glow rings on foundation pads
- Blinking aviation warning lights
- Auto-start simulation on page visit

2D Dashboard Upgrades:
- Draggable topology nodes in React Flow
- Click bus node → detailed right-side inspector panel
- Click transmission line → flow info modal
- Type-specific icons (⚡ generator, ☀️ solar, 🏭 load)
- Animated edges with flow dots and MW labels
- Attack/healing pulse animations on nodes

Data Streaming:
- New SSE hook (useSSEGridData) replaces 1s polling
- Persistent EventSource connection with auto-reconnect
- Shield status now published via EventBus
- Both 2D and 3D pages use SSE

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove double quotes surrounding variable placeholders to prevent copy-paste errors.
- Ensure compliance with website agent rules regarding code blocks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add optional router_weights input to QMoE for separate selection/aggregation routing (#27687)

### Description

Adds optional input `router_weights` (index 14) to `com.microsoft.QMoE`
to decouple Top-K expert selection from output aggregation weighting.

When `router_weights` is provided:
- `router_probs` → Top-K expert selection only
- `router_weights` → values gathered at selected expert indices used as
mixing weights

When omitted, existing softmax-of-`router_probs` behavior is preserved
(backward compatible).

**Changes:**
- **Schema** (`contrib_defs.cc`): New optional input 14
`router_weights`, type T, shape `(num_tokens, num_experts)`
- **CPU provider** (`moe_quantization_cpu.cc`): Implements the separate
routing path with MLFloat16/float support and optional
`normalize_routing_weights` normalization
- **CUDA provider** (`moe_quantization.cc`): Reads input, enforces
not-implemented if provided
- **WebGPU provider** (`qmoe.cc`): Same not-implemented guard
- **Tests** (`moe_test.cc`): `QMoETest_CPU_RouterWeights` covering both
normalized and unnormalized paths with non-zero expected outputs via FC2
bias to validate correct aggregation weights
- **Docs** (`OperatorKernels.md`): Updated CPU and CUDA entries

This pattern matches DeepSeek-V2/V3/R1 routing where `sigmoid(logits)`
is used for aggregation while `logits + bias` with group masking drives
selection:

```python
# DeepSeek-style: different tensors for selection vs aggregation
topk_indices = torch.topk(scores_for_choice, k=top_k)[1]  # selection from modified logits
topk_weights = router_logits.gather(1, topk_indices)        # aggregation from original sigmoid
```

### Motivation and Context

`QMoE` previously required the same tensor for both routing and
weighting, blocking DeepSeek-style `noaux_tc` MoE models where these are
intentionally separate. This unblocks ONNX Runtime export/serving of
DeepSeek-V2/V3/R1 MoE architectures.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[Feature Request] Support noaux_tc MoE routing in
com.microsoft.QMoE via separate router_weights</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Describe the feature request
> 
> `com.microsoft.QMoE` currently accepts a single routing tensor
(commonly router_probs) that is used both for:
> 
> Top‑K expert selection (routing / dispatch), and
> Weighting the outputs of selected experts (aggregation).
> 
> This design makes it impossible to represent DeepSeek‑style `noaux_tc`
`MoE` routing, where different tensors are intentionally used for:
> 
> * expert selection (Top‑K routing), and
> * expert output weighting (mixing).
> 
> This issue proposes adding an optional input `router_weights` to
`com.microsoft.QMoE` so that:
> 
> * `router_probs` is used only for Top‑K selection, and
> * `router_weights` is used only for multiplying / aggregating expert
outputs.
> 
> The change is backward compatible
> This also allows for any other methodology in future where different
tensors are used for selection/aggregation
> 
> ### Describe scenario use case
> 
> Enables exporting and serving DeepSeek‑V2/V3/R1‑style MoE models in
ONNX Runtime</issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>Please update operator spec and implement it in
CPU provider. For CUDA provider, it is fine to throw not implemented
exception for now.
> 
> Example Deepseek MoE script can be found in
https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/75c836b7853cb65f48ab2ce13cddfb12d14ecf5a/src/transformers/models/deepseek_v3/modular_deepseek_v3.py
like the following:
> 
> class DeepseekV3MoE(nn.Module):
>     """
>     A mixed expert module containing shared experts.
>     """
> 
>     def __init__(self, config):
>         super().__init__()
>         self.config = config
>         self.experts = DeepseekV3NaiveMoe(config)
>         self.gate = DeepseekV3TopkRouter(config)
>         self.shared_experts = DeepseekV3MLP(
> config=config, intermediate_size=config.moe_intermediate_size *
config.n_shared_experts
>         )
>         self.n_routed_experts = config.n_routed_experts
>         self.n_group = config.n_group
>         self.topk_group = config.topk_group
>         self.norm_topk_prob = config.norm_topk_prob
>         self.routed_scaling_factor = config.routed_scaling_factor
>         self.top_k = config.num_experts_per_tok
> 
>     def route_tokens_to_experts(self, router_logits):
>         router_logits = router_logits.sigmoid()
> router_logits_for_choice = router_logits +
self.gate.e_score_correction_bias
>         group_scores = (
> router_logits_for_choice.view(-1, self.n_group, self.n_routed_experts
// self.n_group)
>             .topk(2, dim=-1)[0]
>             .sum(dim=-1)
>         )
> group_idx = torch.topk(group_scores, k=self.topk_group, dim=-1,
sorted=False)[1]
>         group_mask = torch.zeros_like(group_scores)
>         group_mask.scatter_(1, group_idx, 1)
>         score_mask = (
>             group_mask.unsqueeze(-1)
> .expand(-1, self.n_group, self.n_routed_experts // self.n_group)
>             .reshape(-1, self.n_routed_experts)
>         )
> scores_for_choice =
router_logits_for_choice.masked_fill(~score_mask.bool(), 0.0)
> topk_indices = torch.topk(scores_for_choice, k=self.top_k, dim=-1,
sorted=False)[1]
>         topk_weights = router_logits.gather(1, topk_indices)
>         if self.norm_topk_prob:
> denominator = topk_weights.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-20
>             topk_weights /= denominator
>         topk_weights = topk_weights * self.routed_scaling_factor
>         return topk_indices, topk_weights
> 
>     def forward(self, hidden_states):
>         residuals = hidden_states
>         orig_shape = hidden_states.shape
>         router_logits = self.gate(hidden_states)
> topk_indices, topk_weights =
self.route_tokens_to_experts(router_logits)
> hidden_states = hidden_states.view(-1, hidden_states.shape[-1])
> hidden_states = self.experts(hidden_states, topk_indices,
topk_weights).view(*orig_shape)
>         hidden_states = hidden_states + self.shared_experts(residuals)
>         return hidden_states
> 
> </agent_instructions>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

feat(terminal): generic terminal notification system (#84)

* Initial plan

* feat(terminal): terminal notification system

Adds a generic keystone.os.notifications module that lets any OS module
register pending-action banners shown at interactive shell login.

- New modules/os/notifications.nix with keystone.os.notifications.items
  option (listOf submodule with id, title, body, markerFile)
- Migrates the TPM enrollment banner from a hard-coded enrollment-check.sh
  script + interactiveShellInit to a notification item registered by tpm.nix
- Imports notifications.nix in modules/os/default.nix
- Removes the now-unused modules/os/scripts/enrollment-check.sh

Desktop integration: the same /var/lib/keystone/ marker files can be polled
by desktop notification daemons for GUI alerts.

* fix(conventions): remove explicit login step from forgejo-project docs

The script auto-authenticates on first use and re-authenticates on

Fix WebGPU ConvTranspose shader bugs for non-vectorizable input channels (#27749)

## Description

Fix two bugs in the WebGPU ConvTranspose shader code generation
(conv_backprop.cc) in the `pack_input_as4_` code path when
`a_components_ == 1` (triggered when input channels per group is not
divisible by 2 or 4, e.g., 5 or 7).

### Bug 1: Wrong offset for weight reads
Weight values were read using `x_offset` (the input/dy tensor offset)
instead of `w_offset` (the weight tensor offset), producing incorrect
convolution results.

### Bug 2: Missing weight multiplication in remainder loop
The remainder loop (handling leftover channels when
`inputChannelsPerGroup` is not a multiple of 4) was adding raw input
values to `dotProd` without multiplying by the corresponding weight
values.

## Motivation and Context

The `inChannels = 5` and `inChannels = 7` test cases in
`conv-transpose.jsonc` were failing because these channel counts aren't
divisible by 2 or 4, triggering the buggy `a_components_ == 1` branch.
Cases like `inChannels = 6` (`a_components_ = 2`) and `inChannels = 8`
(`a_components_ = 4`) were unaffected.

## Testing

All 22 conv-transpose WebGPU tests now pass:
```
npm test -- op conv-transpose.jsonc -b=webgpu -e=node
22 passing (23s)
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Use logical operators instead of any([...]) for lazy evaluation (#253)

* ⚡ Bolt: Use logical operators instead of any([...]) for lazy evaluation

This changes an allocation of a list that's passed to `any()` into a set of standard boolean expressions, saving list allocation overhead and allowing for short-circuiting.

[MLAS] Enable BF16 KAI SBGemm on NCHWc ARM builds (#27703)

### Description
Allow using KAI SBGemm on any ARM64 build (previously it needed the
condition that NCHWc be not enabled as the logistics of using the KAI
SBGemm path for the SBGemm based Conv was not clear). This change
instead allows using the KAI path for other call sites (by enabling it
in the build) but explicitly disallows using the KAI path only for the
Conv and continue using the default MLAS path for the same (as before).



### Motivation and Context

https://github.com/microsoft/onnxruntime/pull/26773#discussion_r2931357688

---------

commit.message:
test: add shared fixtures and improve test infrastructure (#105)

- Add project-wide tests/conftest.py with common path fixtures,
  seed reset, mocks, and skip marks (skip_if_missing_testdata,
  skip_if_no_gpu)
- Implement --use-gpu pytest option via pytest_addoption and
  use_gpu fixture so the gpu_compatible marker is functional
- Guard all CUDA calls in reset_seed behind torch.cuda.is_available()
  to prevent failures in CPU-only environments
- Add CLI-specific tests/mussel/cli/conftest.py with test_data_path_cli
  alias fixture
- Register gpu_compatible marker in pyproject.toml

Addresses review comments:
- No unused heavy imports at collection time
- --use-gpu flag properly registered via pytest_addoption
- CUDA API calls guarded for CPU-only torch installs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial implementation of KG testing harness

- 4 graph types: Simple, Hierarchical, Multi, WeightedGraph
- 5 multi-turn test scenarios (54 total turns)
- Ollama client with qwen3.5:4b + thinking token stripping
- Context manager with sliding window (forces KG use)
- KG-augmented agent with label/tag search and neighbor expansion
- Test runner with keyword accuracy evaluation
- Markdown reporter for per-run and summary reports
- Research matrix builder for full parameter sweep
- Initial quick test: 98.3% accuracy across 54 turns

commit.message:
Potential fix for pacman datetime in list_installed_packages.py

parse_any_datetime_to_epoch() doesn’t currently handle pacman log timestamps like YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (no seconds / no timezone), which are shown in the examples above. This will cause those entries to be skipped (ts=None) and packages won’t appear in the output. Consider extending the parser to accept %Y-%m-%d %H:%M (and optionally %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S) without %z, treating naive times as local time (or explicitly documenting the assumption).

fix: WebAuthn registration for iOS passkeys (Face ID)

- Set ResidentKey to 'preferred' for discoverable credential support
  (required for iOS Passwords/Keychain passkey storage)
- Auto-detect request origin and add to RPOrigins dynamically so
  the origin validation passes when accessing from mobile devices
- Support comma-separated WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN for multiple origins
- Pre-read request body to prevent double-consumption issue
- Include transports and authenticatorAttachment in attestation response
- Add structured error logging for WebAuthn ceremonies

commit.message:
Add minimal wp-env PHPUnit application test scaffolding and developer-oriented docs (#178)

* Initial plan

* feat: add wp-env phpunit bootstrap docs and ci snippet

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: address review feedback on phpunit bootstrap docs

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: restructure testing guide with starlight steps and first test flow

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite testing guide as developer-oriented authoring walkthrough

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: tighten test examples in application testing guide

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden phpunit setup and activate CI test job

- Activate test-application CI job (was commented out)
- Remove deprecated phpunit.xml.dist attributes
- Add tests/php/ExampleTest.php as working smoke test
- Use container phpunit directly in test:php script (no Composer wrapper)
- Restore yoast/phpunit-polyfills as explicit dep (WordPress test bootstrap requirement)
- Remove phpunit/phpunit and wp-phpunit/wp-phpunit (provided by container and polyfills)
- Simplify bootstrap.php: drop non-wp-env fallback, fail fast with clear message
- Update testing docs: wp-env-exclusive constraint, tests-cli rationale, configuration notes

* ci: run application tests after static analysis passes

Exit early benefit: avoid spinning up Docker/wp-env when PHPStan
or lint already failed. Cache keys still resolve via needs: setup.

* docs: update github-actions doc with test-application job and stage structure

* chore: fix gitignore and distignore for phpunit artifacts

- Add phpunit.xml and .phpunit.result.cache to .gitignore
- Add phpunit.xml to .distignore
- Remove stale Cypress test entries from .gitignore

* docs: add plugin dependencies section to testing guide

* feat: add application-testing skill for writing PHPUnit tests

* chore: remove redundant namespace lookup guidance from testing skill

* docs: add application-testing skill to work-with-ai, add factory rollback note to testing

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Justin Vogt <mail@justin-vogt.com>
